
EXAMPLES FOR CHAPTER 2 - "Paths and Painting".

The examples for this chapter require 56 KB.
			
Files contained in this folder:				
	Manifest
	cube.ps
	fill1.ps
	fill2.ps
	fill3.ps
	fill4.ps
	fill5.ps
	fill6.ps (1)
	fill7.ps (1)
	fill8.ps
	fllrule1.ps
	fllrule2.ps
	fllrule3.ps
	fllrule4.ps
	hitdetct.ps (2) (*)
	order1.ps
	order2.ps
	rect1.ps (*)
	rect2.ps (*)
	rect3.ps (*)
	rect4.ps (*)
	rect5.ps (*)
	rect6.ps (3) (*)
	rect7.ps (*)
	rectem1.ps
	rectem2.ps
	rectem3.ps
	rectem4.ps
	rectfll1.ps (*)
	rectfll2.ps (*)
	rectfll3.ps (*)
	rectfll4.ps (*)
	rectfll5.ps (*)
	square.ps
	stroke1.ps
	triangl1.ps
	triangl2.ps
	triangl3.ps
	usrpath1.ps
	usrpath2.ps (*)
	usrpath3.ps (*)
	usrpath4.ps (4) (*)
	usrpath5.ps (4) (*)
	usrpath6.ps (*)
	usrpath7.ps (*)
	usrpath8.ps (*)

(*)	Requires PostScript Level 2.

(1)	In both of these examples, the line reading
		width 0 rlineto			%  bottom
	in the book had an error. This line was modified in these files
	to read:
		width neg 0 rlineto		%  bottom

(2)	In the book the code for hitdetct.ps did not choose a font. The
	following was added to this example to make it work
		/Palatino-Italic findfont	%  choose font
		48 scalefont			%  set font size
		setfont				%  establish current font

	Also, as listed in the book, this example gave different answers
	on different devices. This was because the point chosen to test
	was right on the edge of the circle and some systems consider it
	to be within the current path, and some consider the point outside
	the current path.
	
(3)	The line reading
		2 inch 1 inch			%  lower-left corner
	in the book was changed to read
		1 inch 1 inch			%  lower-left corner
	This gives a truer image to that pictured.
	
(4)	The listing in the book for these examples accidentally omitted
	the definition for the inch procedure. These examples were modified
	to contain that definition so they'd work.
	
